title: Fagus atropunicea
intro: Fagus atropunicea, also known as the Purple Beech, is a species of tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. The Purple Beech has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular and immune system function. The bioactive constituents of Fagus atropunicea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the Purple Beech is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make tea, tinctures, and capsules, which are commonly used to support respiratory health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Fagus atropunicea is a species of beech tree that belongs to the Fagaceae family of plants. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the beech family.
